revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''薬王寺'''（やくおうじ）は、[[千葉県]][[白井市]]清戸にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。山号は青龍山。本尊は[[阿弥陀如来]]。 ...'
revision.text:
'''薬王寺'''（やくおうじ）は、[[千葉県]][[白井市]]清戸にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。山号は青龍山。本尊は[[阿弥陀如来]]。
==由緒== この寺の創建については不詳であるが、言い伝えによれば[[大同 (日本)|大同]]年間（[[806年]]～[[810年]]）という。
==文化財== *千葉県指定史跡 **清戸の泉附版木 :上記のうち版木（「清戸の泉」に祀られる[[弁財天 (白井市)|弁財天社]]にまつわる縁起）がこの寺に保存されている。なお、「清戸の泉」は船橋カントリー倶楽部内にある。
==所在地== *千葉県白井市清戸6
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/select/mokuteki/resource/s_siro_yakuo.html 薬王寺（千葉県の観光まるごと紹介）]
[[category:千葉県の寺|やくおうし]] [[category:天台系仏教|やくおうし]] [[category:白井市|やくおうし]] {{buddhism-stub}}

[[ファイル:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''薬王寺'''（やくおうじ）は、[[千葉県]][[白井市]][[清戸 (白井市)|清戸]]にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。山号は青龍山。本尊は[[阿弥陀如来]]。
==由緒== この寺の創建については不詳であるが、言い伝えによれば[[大同 (日本)|大同]]年間（[[806年]] - [[810年]])に建てられたと伝えられている。 近隣に[[弁財天 (白井市)|弁財天]]という神社がある。 [[ファイル:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|薬師堂]] == 伽藍 == *本堂 **おそらく[[大同 (日本)|大同]]年間に建立されたと思われる。[[2001年]]に再建された。 *薬師堂 ==文化財== *千葉県指定史跡 **清戸の泉附版木 :上記のうち版木（「清戸の泉」に祀られる[[弁財天 (白井市)|弁財天社]]にまつわる縁起）がこの寺に保存されている。なお、「清戸の泉」は船橋カントリー倶楽部内にある。
==所在地== *千葉県白井市清戸6
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|薬王寺}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/select/mokuteki/resource/s_siro_yakuo.html 薬王寺（千葉県の観光まるごと紹介）] * [http://city.shiroi.chiba.jp/webapps/www/service/detail.jsp?id=682 白井市文化財]
{{buddhism-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:やくおうし}} [[Category:天台宗の寺院]] [[Category:千葉県の寺]] [[Category:白井市の歴史]] [[Category:白井市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Created page with "[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Yakuō-ji''' (薬王寺) is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Shiroi, Chiba|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyodo|ja|清戸}} area. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Seiryūzan, and its principal deity is {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}. == History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according..."
revision.text:
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Yakuō-ji''' (薬王寺) is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Shiroi, Chiba|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyodo|ja|清戸}} area. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Seiryūzan, and its principal deity is {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}.
== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Yakushi Hall]]
== Structures == * **Main Hall** Believed to have originally been constructed during the Daidō era. The current structure was rebuilt in 2001. * **Yakushi Hall** Dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}.
== Cultural Properties == * **Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site** **Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks** The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. "Kiyodo no Izumi" itself is located within the grounds of the {{ill|Funabashi Country Club|ja|船橋カントリークラブ}}.
== Location == * 6 Kiyodo, Shiroi, Chiba Prefecture
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|Yakuō-ji}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/select/mokuteki/resource/s_siro_yakuo.html Yakuō-ji (Chiba Prefecture Tourism Guide)] * [http://city.shiroi.chiba.jp/webapps/www/service/detail.jsp?id=682 Shiroi City Cultural Properties]
{{buddhism-stub}}
[[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shiroi, Chiba]] [[Category:History of Shiroi, Chiba]] [[Category:Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[ja:薬王寺 (白井市)]]

[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Yakuō-ji''' (薬王寺) is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Shiroi, Chiba|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyodo|ja|清戸}} area. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Seiryūzan, and its principal deity is {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}.
== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Yakushi Hall]]
== Structures == * **Main Hall** Believed to have originally been constructed during the Daidō era. The current structure was rebuilt in 2001. * **Yakushi Hall** Dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}.
== Cultural Properties == * **Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site** **Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks** The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. "Kiyodo no Izumi" itself is located within the grounds of the {{ill|Funabashi Country Club|ja|船橋カントリークラブ}}.
== Location == * 6 Kiyodo, Shiroi, Chiba Prefecture
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|Yakuō-ji}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/select/mokuteki/resource/s_siro_yakuo.html Yakuō-ji (Chiba Prefecture Tourism Guide)] * [http://city.shiroi.chiba.jp/webapps/www/service/detail.jsp?id=682 Shiroi City Cultural Properties]
{{buddhism-stub}}

[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Yakuō-ji''' (薬王寺) is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Shiroi, Chiba|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyodo|ja|清戸}} area. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Seiryūzan, and its principal deity is {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}.
== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Yakushi Hall]]
== Structures == * **Main Hall** Believed to have originally been constructed during the Daidō era. The current structure was rebuilt in 2001. * **Yakushi Hall** Dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}.
== Cultural Properties == * **Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site** **Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks** The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. "Kiyodo no Izumi" itself is located within the grounds of the {{ill|Funabashi Country Club|ja|船橋カントリークラブ}}.
== Location == * 6 Kiyodo, Shiroi, Chiba Prefecture
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|Yakuō-ji}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/select/mokuteki/resource/s_siro_yakuo.html Yakuō-ji (Chiba Prefecture Tourism Guide)] * [http://city.shiroi.chiba.jp/webapps/www/service/detail.jsp?id=682 Shiroi City Cultural Properties]
{{buddhism-stub}}

revision.text:
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Yakuō-ji''' (薬王寺) is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Shiroi, Chiba|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyodo|ja|清戸}} area. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Seiryūzan, and its principal deity is {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}.
== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Yakushi Hall]]
== Structures == * '''Main Hall''' Believed to have originally been constructed during the Daidō era. The current structure was rebuilt in 2001. * '''Yakushi Hall''' Dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site''' '''Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks''' The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. "Kiyodo no Izumi" itself is located within the grounds of the {{ill|Funabashi Country Club|ja|船橋カントリークラブ}}.
== Location == * 6 Kiyodo, Shiroi, Chiba Prefecture
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|Yakuō-ji}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/select/mokuteki/resource/s_siro_yakuo.html Yakuō-ji (Chiba Prefecture Tourism Guide)] * [http://city.shiroi.chiba.jp/webapps/www/service/detail.jsp?id=682 Shiroi City Cultural Properties]
{{buddhism-stub}}

The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Seiryūzan, and its principal deity is {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}.
== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Yakushi Hall]]
== Structures == * '''Main Hall''' Believed to have originally been constructed during the Daidō era. The current structure was rebuilt in 2001. * '''Yakushi Hall''' Dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site''' '''Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks''' The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. "Kiyodo no Izumi" itself is located within the grounds of the {{ill|Funabashi Country Club|ja|船橋カントリークラブ}}.
== Location == * 6 Kiyodo, Shiroi, Chiba Prefecture
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|Yakuō-ji}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/select/mokuteki/resource/s_siro_yakuo.html Yakuō-ji (Chiba Prefecture Tourism Guide)] * [http://city.shiroi.chiba.jp/webapps/www/service/detail.jsp?id=682 Shiroi City Cultural Properties]
{{buddhism-stub}}

[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Yakuō-ji''' (薬王寺) is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Shiroi, Chiba|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyodo|ja|清戸}} area. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Seiryūzan, and its principal deity is {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}}.
== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)}} era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Yakushi Hall]]
== Structures == * '''Main Hall''' Believed to have originally been constructed during the Daidō era. The current structure was rebuilt in 2001. * '''Yakushi Hall''' Dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site''' '''Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks''' The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. "Kiyodo no Izumi" itself is located within the grounds of the {{ill|Funabashi Country Club|ja|船橋カントリークラブ}}.
== Location == * 6 Kiyodo, Shiroi, Chiba Prefecture
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|Yakuō-ji}}

revision.text:
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Yakuō-ji''' (薬王寺) is a {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗|12=draft}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Shiroi, Chiba|ja|白井市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, in the {{ill|Kiyodo|ja|清戸|12=draft}} area. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Seiryūzan, and its principal deity is {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}}.
== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (日本)|12=draft}} era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=draft}}.
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Yakushi Hall]]
== Structures == * '''Main Hall''' Believed to have originally been constructed during the Daidō era. The current structure was rebuilt in 2001. * '''Yakushi Hall''' Dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|12=draft}}.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site''' '''Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks''' The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=draft}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. "Kiyodo no Izumi" itself is located within the grounds of the {{ill|Funabashi Country Club|ja|船橋カントリークラブ|12=draft}}.
== Location == * 6 Kiyodo, Shiroi, Chiba Prefecture
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|Yakuō-ji}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/select/mokuteki/resource/s_siro_yakuo.html Yakuō-ji (Chiba Prefecture Tourism Guide)] * [http://city.shiroi.chiba.jp/webapps/www/service/detail.jsp?id=682 Shiroi City Cultural Properties]
{{buddhism-stub}} {{citation needed|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)}} <!--Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)-->{{citation needed|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)}} <!--Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)-->{{citation needed|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)}}

23 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:薬王寺 (白井市) up to rev 89969990
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|本堂]] '''薬王寺'''（やくおうじ）は、[[千葉県]][[白井市]][[清戸 (白井市)|清戸]]にある[[天台宗]]の寺院。山号は青龍山。本尊は[[阿弥陀如来]]。
==由緒== この寺の創建については不詳であるが、言い伝えによれば[[大同 (日本)|大同]]年間（[[806年]] - [[810年]])に建てられたと伝えられている。 近隣に[[弁財天 (白井市)|弁財天]]という神社がある。 [[ファイル:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|薬師堂]] == 伽藍 == *本堂 **おそらく[[大同 (日本)|大同]]年間に建立されたと思われる。[[2001年]]に再建された。 *薬師堂 ==文化財== *千葉県指定史跡 **清戸の泉附版木 :上記のうち版木（「清戸の泉」に祀られる[[弁財天 (白井市)|弁財天社]]にまつわる縁起）がこの寺に保存されている。なお、「清戸の泉」は[[船橋カントリークラブ]]内にある。
==所在地== *千葉県白井市清戸6
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|薬王寺}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/select/mokuteki/resource/s_siro_yakuo.html 薬王寺（千葉県の観光まるごと紹介）] * [http://city.shiroi.chiba.jp/webapps/www/service/detail.jsp?id=682 白井市文化財]
{{buddhism-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:やくおうし しろいし}} [[Category:天台宗の寺院]] [[Category:千葉県の寺]] [[Category:白井市の歴史]] [[Category:白井市の建築物]] [[Category:千葉県指定史跡]]

== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the [[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}.

== Cultural Properties == * '''Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site''' **Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks''' The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten Shrine|qq=}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yakuoji (Chiba)]][[ja:薬王寺 (白井市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tendai|WD=Q855964}}|prefecture={{ill|Shiroi|WD=Q519187}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.793116|N|140.090019|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11622490]] Wikidata name: Yakuoji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Yakuō-ji''' (薬王寺) is a [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Shiroi|Shiroi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Kiyodo|ja|清戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyodo|1=Kiyodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279521}} area. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Seiryūzan, and its principal deity is [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]].
== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the [[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}.
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Yakushi Hall]]
== Structures == * '''Main Hall''' Believed to have originally been constructed during the Daidō era. The current structure was rebuilt in 2001. * '''Yakushi Hall''' Dedicated to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]].
== Cultural Properties == * '''Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site''' **Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks''' The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten Shrine|qq=}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. "Kiyodo no Izumi" itself is located within the grounds of the {{ill|Funabashi Country Club|ja|船橋カントリークラブ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funabashi Country Club|qq=}}.
== Location == * 6 Kiyodo, Shiroi, Chiba Prefecture
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|Yakuō-ji}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/select/mokuteki/resource/s_siro_yakuo.html Yakuō-ji (Chiba Prefecture Tourism Guide)] * [http://city.shiroi.chiba.jp/webapps/www/service/detail.jsp?id=682 Shiroi City Cultural Properties]
{{buddhism-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)|version=89969990|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the [[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}.
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Yakushi Hall]]
== Structures == * '''Main Hall''' Believed to have originally been constructed during the Daidō era. The current structure was rebuilt in 2001. * '''Yakushi Hall''' Dedicated to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]].
== Cultural Properties == * '''Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site''' **Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks''' The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten Shrine|qq=}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. "Kiyodo no Izumi" itself is located within the grounds of the {{ill|Funabashi Country Club|ja|船橋カントリークラブ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funabashi Country Club|qq=}}.
== Location == * 6 Kiyodo, Shiroi, Chiba Prefecture
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|Yakuō-ji}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yakuoji (Chiba)]][[ja:薬王寺 (白井市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yakuoji (Chiba)]][[ja:薬王寺 (白井市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yakuoji (Chiba)]][[ja:薬王寺 (白井市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tendai|WD=Q855964}}|prefecture={{ill|Shiroi|WD=Q519187}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.793116|N|140.090019|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11622490]] Wikidata name: Yakuoji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Yakuō-ji''' (薬王寺) is a [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Shiroi|Shiroi, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Kiyodo|ja|清戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyodo|1=Kiyodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279521}} area. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Seiryūzan, and its principal deity is [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]].
== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the [[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}.
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Yakushi Hall]]
== Structures == * '''Main Hall''' Believed to have originally been constructed during the Daidō era. The current structure was rebuilt in 2001. * '''Yakushi Hall''' Dedicated to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]].
== Cultural Properties == * '''Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site''' **Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks''' The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten Shrine|qq=}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. "Kiyodo no Izumi" itself is located within the grounds of the {{ill|Funabashi Country Club|ja|船橋カントリークラブ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funabashi Country Club|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yakuoji (Chiba)]][[ja:薬王寺 (白井市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yakuoji (Chiba)]][[ja:薬王寺 (白井市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yakuoji (Chiba)]][[ja:薬王寺 (白井市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tendai|WD=Q855964}}|prefecture={{ill|Shiroi|WD=Q519187}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.793116|N|140.090019|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11622490]] Wikidata name: Yakuoji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Yakuō-ji''' (薬王寺) is a {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Shiroi|en|Shiroi|WD=Q519187|lt=Shiroi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kiyodo|ja|清戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyodo|1=Kiyodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279521}} area. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Seiryūzan, and its principal deity is {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}}.
== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}.
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Yakushi Hall]]
== Structures == * '''Main Hall''' Believed to have originally been constructed during the Daidō era. The current structure was rebuilt in 2001. * '''Yakushi Hall''' Dedicated to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha}}.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site''' **Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks''' The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten Shrine|qq=}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. "Kiyodo no Izumi" itself is located within the grounds of the {{ill|Funabashi Country Club|ja|船橋カントリークラブ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funabashi Country Club|qq=}}.
== Location == * 6 Kiyodo, Shiroi, Chiba Prefecture
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|Yakuō-ji}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/select/mokuteki/resource/s_siro_yakuo.html Yakuō-ji (Chiba Prefecture Tourism Guide)] * [http://city.shiroi.chiba.jp/webapps/www/service/detail.jsp?id=682 Shiroi City Cultural Properties]
{{buddhism-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yakuoji (Chiba)]][[ja:薬王寺 (白井市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yakuoji (Chiba)]][[ja:薬王寺 (白井市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Yakuoji (Chiba)]][[ja:薬王寺 (白井市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tendai|WD=Q855964|1=Tendai}}|prefecture={{ill|Shiroi|WD=Q519187|1=Shiroi}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.793116|N|140.090019|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} [[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-main-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Yakuō-ji''' (薬王寺) is a {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai|1=Tiantai}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Shiroi|en|Shiroi|WD=Q519187|lt=Shiroi, Chiba|1=Shiroi}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kiyodo|ja|清戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyodo|1=Kiyodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279521}} area. The temple’s mountain name (''sangō'') is Seiryūzan, and its principal deity is {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}}.
== History == The exact date of Yakuō-ji’s founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it was established during the {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} era (806–810 CE). Nearby, there is a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}.
[[File:Yakuou-ji-temple-yakushi-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Yakushi Hall]]
== Structures == * '''Main Hall''' Believed to have originally been constructed during the Daidō era. The current structure was rebuilt in 2001. * '''Yakushi Hall''' Dedicated to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Chiba Prefecture Designated Historic Site''' **Kiyodo no Izumi with Woodblocks''' The woodblocks, which are related to the origin story of the {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten Shrine|qq=}} enshrined at "Kiyodo no Izumi," are preserved at this temple. "Kiyodo no Izumi" itself is located within the grounds of the {{ill|Funabashi Country Club|ja|船橋カントリークラブ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funabashi Country Club|qq=}}.
== Location == * 6 Kiyodo, Shiroi, Chiba Prefecture
{{commonscat|Yakuoji (Chiba)|Yakuō-ji}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/select/mokuteki/resource/s_siro_yakuo.html Yakuō-ji (Chiba Prefecture Tourism Guide)] * [http://city.shiroi.chiba.jp/webapps/www/service/detail.jsp?id=682 Shiroi City Cultural Properties]
{{buddhism-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)|version=89969990|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:薬王寺 (白井市)]]
